hazards.name: Noctis Chasma
hazards.description: A branch of Noctis Labyrinthus splits the route — a rubble-choked chasm too wide to roll across blind. The read is its span at the narrowest gap your survey found.

options.label: Ford the ramp
options.blurb: Gun a regolith ramp at the narrowest point. Fast and free — but a steep read can pitch the rover.

outcomes.log: The rover noses down the ramp and powers clean across the gap.
A wheel drops into a fissure; you claw out, but the chassis takes a beating.
The ramp gives way. The rover slews into the chasm wall — crew thrown, cargo scattered.

options.label: Bridge it
options.blurb: Deploy spanning plates across the gap. Costs Parts; a wide read risks a plate buckling mid-span.

outcomes.log: The plates hold. The rover crawls across the span and onto solid regolith.
A plate sags under load — you make it, but a crate tumbles into the dark.
The span buckles. Plates and a cargo pallet plunge into the chasm.

options.label: Detour the rim
options.blurb: Follow the chasm rim to a natural land bridge. Safe, but it costs Sols and power on the long way around.

outcomes.log: The rim track is slow but sound. You rejoin the route well past the chasm.

options.label: Winch descent
options.blurb: Pay a rim prospector in rations to talk you down a known route. Near-safe, but you trade away supplies.

outcomes.log: The prospector's beta is perfect. You descend, cross the floor, and winch out the far side.
A winch anchor slips on the descent — recoverable, but it rattles the crew.

hazards.name: Tharsis Storm Wall
hazards.description: A regional dust front stands across the route, a brown wall climbing past the horizon. The read is its density — how blind you'll be and how hard it abrades.

options.label: Punch through
options.blurb: Drive the storm on instruments. Fast — but a dense read crashes power and abrades the hull.

outcomes.log: You thread the front on dead reckoning and break into clear air beyond.
Grit chews the seals and the panels brown out — you crawl through on reserve power.
Navigation whites out. You wander off-route for a Sol before the storm clears.

options.label: Hunker down
options.blurb: Park, seal up, and wait the front out. Safe — but it costs Sols and risks a dig-out.

outcomes.log: The front passes overnight. You shake off the dust and roll on.
The rover half-buries in drift. The crew spends a Sol digging it free.

options.label: Reroute wide
options.blurb: Skirt the storm's edge on a long arc. Reliable, but the detour eats distance and power.

outcomes.log: The wide arc keeps you in clear air the whole way around the front.

hazards.name: Marineris Dust Pit
hazards.description: Fine fluffy regolith pools in a basin like quicksand — the rover sinks if it stops. The read is the pit's depth: how far you'd bog.

options.label: Power through
options.blurb: Keep momentum and drive straight across. Free and fast — but a deep read bogs you completely.

outcomes.log: Wheels spinning, you skim across the pit before it can swallow them.
The rover bogs to the axles. Hours of winching and digging to break free.

options.label: Lay traction mats
options.blurb: Roll out mats ahead of the wheels. Costs Parts, but it's reliable across any depth.

outcomes.log: The mats bite. You crawl across the pit on a firm track and recover them the far side.
A mat sinks before you recover it — left behind in the dust.

options.label: Skirt the edge
options.blurb: Trace the firm rim around the basin. Safe, but the long way costs Sols.

outcomes.log: The rim holds firm the whole way. Slow, but you never come close to bogging.

hazards.name: Dichotomy Ice Sheet
hazards.description: A buried glacier spans the dichotomy boundary, its surface a dirty white shelf. The read is the ice thickness — thin spots break through like a river ford gone wrong.

options.label: Drive across
options.blurb: Roll straight over the shelf. Fast — but a thin read means a wheel punches through.

outcomes.log: The shelf holds firm under the rover's weight. A clean, fast crossing.
Ice cracks underfoot — a wheel drops through into slush before you gun clear.
The shelf gives way. The rover drops a half-metre into a melt void — a hard, cold recovery.

options.label: Probe a route
options.blurb: Send the crew ahead to sound the ice and flag a thick path. Costs Sols, far safer.

outcomes.log: Probing finds a thick lobe. You follow the flags across without a creak.
Even the flagged path thins midway — a tense, slow crawl, but you make it.

options.label: Go around
options.blurb: Detour to where the glacier pinches out. Long, but it never tests the ice.

outcomes.log: You round the glacier's snout on bare rock and rejoin the route.

hazards.name: Korolev Scarp
hazards.description: The basin rim drops away in a cliff scarp on the final approach to Korolev. The read is the descent grade — how steep the way down.

options.label: Direct descent
options.blurb: Take the cliff head-on. Fast — but a steep read risks the rover and the crew.

outcomes.log: Brakes smoking, you ride the grade straight down to the basin floor.
The rover slides the last stretch and slams the apron — jarring, but intact enough.
A wheel station shears on the descent. You reach the floor battered and a crew bruised.

options.label: Switchback
options.blurb: Pick a zig-zag track down the scarp. Slow and Sol-costly, but sound.

outcomes.log: The switchbacks are tedious but stable. You roll onto the basin floor unscathed.

options.label: Lower with winch
options.blurb: Anchor and winch the rover down in stages. Costs Parts, but it tames any grade.

outcomes.log: Stage by stage, the winch lowers the rover to the floor. Slow, controlled, clean.
An anchor pulls on the second stage — a heart-stopping drop, then it catches.
